meta.title: Nassila — Verify your references. Ground your claims.
meta.description: The last check before you submit — bibliography, registries, and source-backed writing. Desktop app for Windows.

home.heroTagline: Verify your references. Ground your claims.
home.heroSubtitle: The last check before you submit — bibliography, registries, and source-backed writing.
home.tryItHint: Plain-text parser preview (offline, in your browser)
home.tryPlaceholder: Smith J, Doe A. Climate adaptation in coastal cities. Nature. 2020;586(7829):123-128. doi:10.1038/s41586-020-1234-5
home.noParse: Could not parse — try a DOI or Vancouver-style journal line
home.loopSubtitle: One connected manuscript workflow — not seven disconnected tools.
home.featuresSubtitle: A quality pass on citations you already have — not a reference manager replacement.

loop.uploadDesc: DOCX, PDF, or paste text — extract structure and in-text citations.
loop.sourcesDesc: Attach PDFs or resolve via Crossref, PubMed, and OpenAlex.
loop.auditDesc: L1/L2 registry verify plus Sanad passage grounding with a local LLM.
loop.explainDesc: Mismatch cards and risk flags with jump-to-row clarity.
loop.exportDesc: Formatted bibliography in thousands of CSL styles.

import.title: Import or paste
import.body: BibTeX, RIS, CSL-JSON, plain text, DOCX/PDF reference sections, or exports from Zotero, Mendeley, and EndNote.

validate.title: Validate offline
validate.body: CSL validation, autocorrect for DOI formats, capitalization, and page ranges — no network required.

verify.title: Verify L1 + L2
verify.body: Resolve each row to Crossref, PubMed, or OpenAlex, then compare your metadata to the canonical record. Up to 200 rows per run.

integrity.title: Integrity checks
integrity.body: Predatory journal flags, duplicate groups with merge, and honest registry mismatch cards.

manuscript.title: Manuscript loop
manuscript.body: Upload a draft, verify cited references, fetch open-access source text where available, and run Sanad grounding — AI-assisted; verify manually.

export.title: Export anywhere
export.body: APA, IEEE, Vancouver, Chicago, Harvard, MLA, Nature, or any style from the Zotero CSL repository.

download.title: Download
download.subtitle: Windows installer from GitHub Releases. Editing and validation work offline.
download.reqRam: 4 GB RAM minimum; 8 GB recommended for manuscript audit with local LLM
download.reqDisk: ~200 MB for the app; additional space for LM Studio models if using Sanad
download.installSteps: Download the installer, run Nassila Setup, and launch from the Start menu. No account required.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.offlineBody: List editing, validation, formatting, and deduplication do not require a network connection.
privacy.networkBody: Registry verification, DOI lookup, predatory-list sync, open-access source fetch, and optional LLM calls run only when you trigger those actions.
privacy.localBody: Sanad grounding talks to LM Studio, Ollama, or another endpoint you configure — not a cloud default.

about.title: About Nassila
about.sanad: The name Nassila is coined, inspired by a sanad (سند): a clear chain from what you write to where it came from.
about.notRm: Nassila is not a full reference manager. It is a quality pass before submission: validate, verify, dedupe, flag predatory journals, export.
about.ai: AI-assisted — never "AI writes your citations." Deterministic layers decide where schemas and registries are authoritative.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.subtitle: Notable releases of the desktop app, with worker-themed codenames where applicable.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.indexIntro: Exhaustive user-facing reference for Nassila — bibliography workflows, manuscript audit, Sanad setup, verification tiers, and roadmap.
docs.releaseTrainIntro: Nassila ships on a versioned schedule with worker-themed codenames. App version (e.g. 1.1.2) is separate from Sanad checkpoint labels (S12, S14).

footer.tagline: Verify your references. Ground your claims.
